title: Agent Adapter Contract
description: Contract schema for external coding agent runtimes (Claude Code CLI, Codex, Cursor, GitHub Copilot cloud agent, gh CLI PR connector, custom CLI/HTTP) integrating with ao-kernel. An adapter is the bridge between ao-kernel workflows and an agent runtime. This contract defines the invocation envelope, input/output shapes, capability declaration, interrupt semantics, and policy/evidence wiring. Referential integrity with workflow-run (run_id) and policy_worktree_profile (policy_refs) is narrative at PR-A0; loader-level validation lands in Tranche A PR-A2.

adapter_id.description: Unique adapter slug. Lowercase letter, followed by 2-63 lowercase letters, digits, or dashes. Must be unique within a workspace's adapter registry.

version.description: Adapter package version in SemVer 2.0 format (e.g. '1.0.0', '2.3.0-rc.1').

capabilities.description: Declared capabilities the adapter supports. Subset of: read_repo, write_diff, run_tests, open_pr, human_interrupt, stream_output. Note: commit_write and branch_create are NOT capabilities — git commit and branch operations are ao-kernel's responsibility (worktree executor + PR orchestrator). MCP/tool access is NOT a capability — it is policy-gated via policy_refs.

input_envelope.description: Shape of the payload ao-kernel sends to the adapter when invoking it.

output_envelope.description: Shape of the payload the adapter returns to ao-kernel after completing or partially completing the task.

interrupt_contract.description: Optional. Defines how the adapter surfaces human-in-the-loop requests mid-run. Required if capabilities includes 'human_interrupt'.

policy_refs.description: Workspace-relative references to policy files that must be loaded before this adapter is invoked. Must include 'ao_kernel/defaults/policies/policy_worktree_profile.v1.json' at minimum. Optional: policy_tool_calling.v1.json (if adapter invokes MCP tools), policy_mcp_memory.v1.json (if adapter reads canonical memory).

evidence_refs.description: JSONL path patterns where adapter invocations emit evidence events. Standard pattern: '.ao/evidence/workflows/{run_id}/adapter-{adapter_id}.jsonl'. Multiple patterns allowed for adapters that write to additional evidence surfaces (e.g., SHA256 integrity manifest paths under '.ao/evidence/workflows/{run_id}/manifest.json').

output_parse.description: Optional, capability-aware typed-payload extraction surface (FAZ-B PR-B0). NET-NEW contract surface, distinct from invocation_http.response_parse. response_parse is transport-level: it maps an HTTP response body into the output_envelope shape. output_parse is orchestrator-level: after the envelope is in hand, the rule walker in adapter_invoker._invocation_from_envelope extracts named payloads from the envelope and validates each against a capability-specific schema. Adapters that do not declare output_parse behave as before; adapters that declare it advertise a typed extraction contract the orchestrator honours at invocation time. See docs/BENCHMARK-SUITE.md §3 for the rule shape and edge-case contracts.

adapter_kind_enum.description: Adapter category. 'claude-code-cli': Anthropic Claude Code CLI subprocess. 'codex-cli': OpenAI Codex CLI. 'codex-stub': in-process deterministic stub for tests/demos. 'github-copilot-cloud': GitHub Copilot cloud agent (HTTP). 'cursor-bg': Cursor background agent (HTTP). 'gh-cli-pr': typed VCS/PR connector wrapping 'gh' CLI for PR creation — NOT a full coding agent. 'custom-cli' / 'custom-http': escape hatch for user-defined adapters. Promotion criteria for new enum values: (a) a bundled adapter manifest ships in ao_kernel/defaults/, OR (b) 3+ independent user requests without workspace override. Candidates currently routed via custom-* escape hatch: aider-cli, devin-http, windsurf-cascade.

capability_enum.description: Atomic capability the adapter can perform. read_repo: adapter may read files from the workspace view. write_diff: adapter may produce a unified diff as output. run_tests: adapter may execute the workspace test suite in its worktree. open_pr: adapter may itself create a PR (e.g., GitHub Copilot cloud agent); if absent, ao-kernel opens the PR via gh-cli-pr adapter. human_interrupt: adapter may mid-run request human input via interrupt_contract. stream_output: adapter streams partial output chunks rather than returning a single response. review_findings: adapter produces a typed review-findings artifact (see review-findings.schema.v1.json) extracted via the output_parse rule-walker in adapter_invoker; required for the governed_review benchmark workflow. commit_message: adapter produces a typed commit-message artifact (see commit-message.schema.v1.json) PR-B6; operator downstream applies the actual git commit — ao-kernel NEVER executes git commit itself (NOT `commit_write`).

transport.description: Invocation transport. 'cli': subprocess-based. 'http': network endpoint. grpc/websocket are out of scope at v1 — promotion gated by 2 bundled adapters or explicit demand.

invocation.description: Transport-dispatched invocation spec. Exactly one branch (cli or http) must validate.

command.description: Executable to invoke. Must resolve under policy_worktree_profile.command_allowlist (exact or prefix match).

args.description: Argument template. Placeholders like '{task_prompt}', '{context_pack_ref}', '{run_id}' are substituted at invocation time. Secrets are NOT substituted into args (policy_worktree_profile.secrets.denied_exposure_modes includes 'argv').

env_allowlist_ref.description: Pointer to policy_worktree_profile.env_allowlist keys. Typically '#/env_allowlist/allowed_keys' (entire list) or a subset.

cwd_policy.description: Working directory policy. per_run_worktree: fresh worktree per run (default). shared_readonly: shared repo read-only mount (for analysis-only adapters).

stdin_mode.description: stdin usage. none: no stdin. prompt_only: task_prompt written to stdin (non-secret). multipart: structured JSONL on stdin (input_envelope). Secrets NEVER written to stdin (policy_worktree_profile.secrets.denied_exposure_modes includes 'stdin').

exit_code_map.description: Optional map from adapter exit code (as string key) to output_envelope.status. Default: 0 -> ok, non-zero -> failed.

endpoint.description: HTTP endpoint URL template. May contain placeholders like '{run_id}'.

auth_secret_id_ref.description: Secret ID referenced in policy_worktree_profile.secrets.allowlist_secret_ids. The resolved secret value is injected only into HTTP headers (not URL, not body) via the provider's documented auth scheme. policy_worktree_profile.secrets.denied_exposure_modes prohibits http_header default; http adapters must set exposure_modes to include 'http_header' explicitly under workspace override.

headers_allowlist.description: Non-auth header names the adapter is allowed to set. Auth header is derived from auth_secret_id_ref; other secrets must not appear.

request_body_template.description: JSON body template. Placeholders like '{task_prompt}', '{context_pack_ref}' substituted at invocation.

diff_jsonpath.description: JSONPath to unified diff in response body.

error_jsonpath.description: JSONPath to error payload (on non-ok response).

response_parse.description: Optional response parsing hints for HTTP adapters whose payload shape differs from output_envelope.

task_prompt.description: Natural-language task description (e.g., the issue body, spec markdown, or bug report). Non-secret by construction.

context_pack_ref.description: Workspace-relative path to the compiled context pack (canonical decisions + session transcript + workspace facts). Adapter reads this file directly; adapter does NOT need context_pack_read capability — context delivery is via input envelope, not a privilege.

workspace_view.description: Optional file allowlist defining what the adapter may read from the worktree.

run_id.description: Workflow run UUID. Narrative reference to workflow-run.schema.v1.json::run_id; registry-level validation lands in Tranche A PR-A2.

allowlist_globs.description: Glob patterns the adapter may read. Default recommendation: ['**/*.py', '**/*.md', 'pyproject.toml'] for Python projects.

denylist_globs.description: Globs explicitly excluded. Applied after allowlist. Default recommendation: ['.ao/**', '.git/**', '**/.env*', '**/secrets.*'].

max_total_bytes.description: Total read budget across all workspace view reads (bytes). Default 16 MiB.

tokens.description: LLM token ceiling (adapter input + output tokens combined).

fail_closed_on_exhaust.description: MUST be true. When any budget axis reaches its limit, further invocation is denied (CNS-007 fail-closed pattern; TRANCHE-STRATEGY-V2.md §3 hard budget cap).

status.description: Top-level outcome. ok: adapter returned a valid response. declined: adapter refused (policy, safety, scope). interrupted: adapter requested human input mid-run (interrupt_token set). failed: invocation or parse error (error field set). partial: adapter streamed some output but did not finish (finish_reason set). Note: 'timeout' is NOT a top-level status — timeouts surface as status=partial with finish_reason=timeout, or status=failed with error.category=timeout (repo precedent: tests/test_stream_post_processors.py, tests/test_client.py).

diff.description: Unified diff (git format) when status in [ok, partial] and capabilities includes write_diff. Empty string when no change proposed.

commands_executed.description: Per-command execution records. Subject to policy_worktree_profile.evidence_redaction rules.

logs_ref.description: Workspace-relative path to the adapter's redacted log JSONL. Matches evidence_refs pattern.

evidence_events.description: Evidence events the adapter wants ao-kernel to emit to the workflow timeline.

error.description: Populated only when status=failed. category enumerates recoverable vs terminal failure modes.

finish_reason.description: Applies to status in [ok, partial]. Mirrors provider-native finish_reason semantics.

interrupt_token.description: Populated only when status=interrupted. Opaque token used by ao-kernel to resume the adapter. Distinct from governance approval_token (separate domain).

cost_actual.description: Actual resource consumption reported by the adapter.

args_shape.description: Redacted argument shape description (e.g., '<path> <hash>'). Raw args are NEVER logged if they may contain secrets.

stdout_ref.description: Path to redacted stdout JSONL, if captured.

stderr_ref.description: Path to redacted stderr JSONL, if captured.

kind.description: One of the 17 event kinds defined in docs/EVIDENCE-TIMELINE.md §2.

event_id.description: ULID. If absent, ao-kernel mints one at emission.

payload_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the event payload (for integrity).

payload_ref.description: Pointer to the full payload if too large to inline (JSONL offset, file path, etc.).

message.description: Human-readable error message. Subject to redaction rules.

category.description: Failure category. Consumers key recovery logic off this, not the code. timeout: wall-clock or provider-side deadline exceeded. invocation_failed: command could not start (PATH miss, port closed, auth failed). output_parse_failed: adapter returned malformed payload. policy_denied: pre-invocation policy check refused. budget_exhausted: any budget axis ran out mid-run. adapter_crash: process terminated abnormally (signal, OOM).

resume_protocol.description: How ao-kernel resumes the adapter. subprocess_stdin: write resume payload to adapter stdin (same process). http_continuation: POST resume payload to endpoint with interrupt_token header. replay_with_reply: adapter is re-invoked with resume_payload appended to input_envelope.task_prompt.

resume_payload_schema_ref.description: Optional ref to a sub-schema describing the expected resume payload shape.

timeout_seconds.description: How long ao-kernel holds the interrupt open before transitioning workflow-run.state to failed (category=timeout).

interrupt_token.description: Opaque resume token. Distinct from governance approval_token.

cost_record.description: Adapter-reported actual resource consumption. Reconciled against budget on return.

format.description: Diff format. 'unified': POSIX unified diff. 'git': git-format patch with commit metadata (prefer when open_pr capability is declared).

base_revision.description: Optional base git SHA the diff was computed against.

diff_patch.description: Structured diff container. output_envelope.diff may carry the raw payload directly; this $def is for adapters that want to send metadata alongside the patch.

rules.description: Ordered list of capability-aware extraction rules. The orchestrator (adapter_invoker._invocation_from_envelope) walks this list after the envelope is in hand and populates InvocationResult.extracted_outputs[capability] with each rule's validated payload. Multiple rules targeting the same capability MUST be rejected as invalid-manifest at load-time (edge-case contract: docs/BENCHMARK-SUITE.md §3.2); this uniqueness is enforced by loader validation, not schema-level constraint (closed-enum + uniqueItems does not cover 'same capability value across different rule objects').

output_parse.description: Net-new typed-payload extraction surface (PR-B0). Optional; adapters that omit it behave as before.

json_path.description: JSONPath pointing into the adapter's output envelope. PR-A3 minimal subset ONLY: '$.key(.key)*'; no array indices, no wildcards, no filters. Non-conforming paths fail schema validation at manifest load. Matches the subset the existing adapter_invoker._jsonpath_dotted already honours.

capability.description: Optional. If present, the rule MUST match one of the capabilities the adapter also declares at top-level 'capabilities[]' (loader cross-ref); the extractor keys results by this name in InvocationResult.extracted_outputs. If absent, the rule is descriptive-only — the orchestrator extracts the payload but does not key it by a capability, and downstream artifact-write logic treats the rule as unnamed (informational).

schema_ref.description: Optional. Path to a JSON Schema for typed payload validation. Resolution order: (1) bundled 'ao_kernel/defaults/schemas/<name>', (2) workspace override '.ao/schemas/<name>'. Unresolvable schema_ref MUST fail-closed: preferred at manifest load-time (AdapterManifestCorruptedError); worst case at invocation path (AdapterOutputParseError → workflow error.category=output_parse_failed). Validation failure on the extracted payload raises the same output_parse_failed category. Edge cases — null payload, missing json_path, duplicate capability, envelope-has-field-but-no-rule — are contract-pinned in docs/BENCHMARK-SUITE.md §3.2.
